And of course, once he crossed the line into some level of celebrity, then the police departments don't care about being embarrassed. He's been on Regis' show more than anybody else has ever been on Regis. All the Regis shows added together. And the thing he always does on the Regis show, and James, is they hide money or something somewhere out in the city in envelopes. And then Kreskin comes on and reads their minds and then he goes out and he finds it all. Right? That's, you know, the deal. And, I mean, he's doing magic is what he's doing. But, to make any reference, he's honed it to a fine art. It is amazing. It is mesmerizing. And it goes all the way back. He used to do it to put people in rooms. Right? So he'd camp out in the city for a week and do that every place you could do it. That there might be 20 or 30 or 40 people. And then get them all in a room and they'd all pay 20 bucks to come in the door. And that's how he made his living. But he also had his time. I mean, he had five bestselling books and Times List. And, you know, he was a big thing for a long period of time. Now, that's all dramatic demonstration aimed more towards the media. So let's talk about dramatic demonstration for the public. Right? And those of you who've done it, it's dramatic. And it has an impact on people. And people talk about it. And people tell others about it.
